name: Hal McIntyre
abstract: Hal McIntyre (born Harold William McIntyre; November 29, 1914, Cromwell, Connecticut – May 5, 1959, Los Angeles, California) was an American saxophonist, clarinetist, and bandleader. McIntyre played extensively as a teenager and led his own octet in 1935.

about_quotes.speaker: Teddy Wilson
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] : Hal McIntyre and Kenton. [INTERVIEWEE] Yeah. Well, Kenton would be the more dominant force...

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: And there was another guy named Hal McIntyre. I used to love the way he played lead alto with his band.

about_quotes.speaker: Horace Henderson
about_quotes.evidence: I had the pleasure of doing a few things for Hal McIntyre... So I made a couple of things for him.

about_quotes.speaker: Joe Segal
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] ...Hal McIntyre, a lot of musicians... [INTERVIEWEE] I didn’t meet any famous ones.
about_quotes.doc_title: Joe Segal — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: John Best
about_quotes.evidence: Tex was in the band. Al Klink, Willie Schwartz, Hal McIntyre.
about_quotes.doc_title: John Best interviewed by Monk Rowe, San Diego, California, February 15, 1998
